parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Owning user to list agents for.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of agents to return. The server may cap the value.

post.summary:
CreateAgent registers a new sub-agent definition for a user.
Owner write.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CreateAgentRequest is the request message for CreateAgent.

get.summary: GetAgent reads an agent by id.

delete.summary: DeleteAgent removes an agent. Owner write.

patch.summary: UpdateAgent modifies fields on an existing agent. Owner write.

get.summary:
ListConnectors returns the connector catalog with per-user
connection state. Visible to any authenticated caller; the
`connected` field is populated only when `user_id` is set on the
request.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description:
Optional user whose per-connector connection state should be
reflected in the `connected` field of each entry. When empty,
`connected` is always false.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of connectors to return.

post.summary:
CreateConnector registers a new MCP connector. Admin-only —
connectors are shared organizational state.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
CreateConnectorRequest is the request message for CreateConnector.
Admin-only; writes a shared organizational resource.

delete.summary:
DeleteConnector removes a connector and cascades to delete every
user's stored credentials for it. Admin-only.

post.summary:
CompleteConnectorOAuthFlow exchanges the authorization code
returned by the OAuth provider for per-user tokens, which are
stored in Secrets under the implementation's convention. OAuth
connectors only.

DisconnectConnector deletes the caller user's stored credential
for a connector. Works for both auth types.

SetConnectorStaticToken stores a user-supplied bearer token for
a static-token connector. Static-token connectors only.

StartConnectorOAuthFlow returns the URL the caller should
redirect the user's browser to. OAuth connectors only.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of skills to return.

post.summary: CreateSkill registers a new skill for a user. Owner write.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CreateSkillRequest is the request message for CreateSkill.

get.summary: GetSkill reads a skill by id.

delete.summary: DeleteSkill removes a skill. Owner write.

patch.summary: UpdateSkill modifies fields on an existing skill. Owner write.

get.summary:
GetUserConfig returns a user's materialized runtime configuration
— agents, skills, and the MCP server configs for every connector
the user has successfully authorized. Workers call this once at
run start. Credentials come from Secrets at resolve time; the
returned `mcp_servers[*].headers` entries carry the live tokens.

userId.description:
User whose flow this completes. Implementations verify this
matches the user encoded in `state`.

code.description:
Authorization code the OAuth provider sent back to the redirect
URL.

state.description:
State token from StartConnectorOAuthFlow. Must match to prevent
CSRF.

redirectUrl.description: The same redirect URL passed to StartConnectorOAuthFlow.

ConfigCompleteConnectorOAuthFlowBody.description:
CompleteConnectorOAuthFlowRequest is the request message for
CompleteConnectorOAuthFlow. Invoked by the implementation's HTTP
OAuth callback endpoint after the user returns from the provider.

userId.description: User whose credential should be cleared.

ConfigDisconnectConnectorBody.description:
DisconnectConnectorRequest is the request message for
DisconnectConnector. Works for both auth types — deletes the
per-user OAuth credential or static token.

token.description:
The bearer token the user supplies. Stored in Secrets under the
per-user convention.

ConfigSetConnectorStaticTokenBody.description:
SetConnectorStaticTokenRequest is the request message for
SetConnectorStaticToken. Applicable only to static-token connectors.

redirectUrl.description:
URL the OAuth provider will redirect to after authorization. The
implementation's OAuth callback handler must be reachable at
this URL.

ConfigStartConnectorOAuthFlowBody.description:
StartConnectorOAuthFlowRequest is the request message for
StartConnectorOAuthFlow. Applicable only to OAuth connectors.

name.description:
Optional replacement name. Must remain unique within the owner's
collection.

tools.description:
Optional replacement tool list. Sent in full; partial merges are
not supported.

setTools.description:
Whether `tools` is being set (true) or left alone (false). Needed
to distinguish "clear the tool list" (set=true, tools empty) from
"leave the tool list" (set=false).

ConfigUpdateAgentBody.description:
UpdateAgentRequest is the request message for UpdateAgent. Unset
fields are preserved.

ConfigUpdateSkillBody.description:
UpdateSkillRequest is the request message for UpdateSkill. Unset
fields are preserved.

userId.description:
Owning user. Agents are personal resources and are not visible to
other users.

name.description:
Agent identifier. Unique within the owner's collection. Used as
the dictionary key when the worker hands agents to the SDK.

description.description:
Human-readable description. The agent SDK routes delegated work
to the sub-agent whose description best matches the intent, so
this text is load-bearing.

prompt.description: System prompt the sub-agent runs under.

tools.description:
Tools the sub-agent is allowed to use. Must be a subset of the
parent agent's allowed tools at run time; Config does not enforce
this — the worker does.

model.description:
Model identifier (for example `"haiku"`, `"sonnet"`, `"opus"`).
Empty means the run-level default.

createdAt.description: Time the agent was first created.

updatedAt.description: Time the agent was most recently updated.

v1Agent.description:
Agent is a per-user sub-agent definition. At run start, Workers
receive the user's agents via GetUserConfig and pass them to the
agent SDK programmatically (no filesystem writes needed).

v1AgentDefinition.description:
AgentDefinition is the shape a worker consumes when handing a
sub-agent to the agent SDK at run time. It is a strict subset of
Agent with `id`, `user_id`, and timestamps stripped.

v1CompleteConnectorOAuthFlowResponse.description:
CompleteConnectorOAuthFlowResponse is the response message for
CompleteConnectorOAuthFlow. Intentionally empty — on success the
per-user credential is stored in Secrets under the implementation's
convention and GetUserConfig will surface the connector in the
user's `mcp_servers`.

name.description:
Display identifier used in URLs and user-facing labels. Unique
across the control plane.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the connector provides.

authType.description:
How this connector authenticates per-user — OAuth vs static
token.

mcpUrl.description:
Remote MCP server URL. The transport (HTTP vs SSE) is derived
from this URL at resolve time; see `config.md` §7.

mcpAllowedTools.description:
Tool name wildcards the agent is allowed to invoke on this MCP
server (for example `["mcp__github__*"]`). Passed through to the
agent SDK's allowed-tools list when the user has authorized the
connector.

oauthScopes.description:
OAuth-only: comma-separated scopes the authorization flow
requests. Empty for static-token connectors.

authProviderRef.description:
Optional implementation-specific hint pointing at the Secrets
backend's representation of the OAuth app registration (for
example an OpenBao `oauthapp` server name). Implementations that
don't need this leave it empty. Not meaningful for static-token
connectors.

createdAt.description: Time the connector was first created.

updatedAt.description: Time the connector was most recently updated.

mcpDisallowedTools.description:
Tool names the agent must NOT invoke. Passed through to the agent
SDK's `disallowed_tools` list. Use exact fully-qualified names
like `["mcp__dropbox__list_folder"]` — suffix wildcards do NOT
match exact names (verified against claude-agent-sdk 0.1.39).
Use to bypass upstream MCP tools whose JSON Schema is rejected by
the Anthropic Messages API (top-level oneOf/allOf/anyOf).

v1Connector.description:
Connector is an admin-managed MCP server registry entry. One row
per connector name (for example `"github"`, `"linear"`,
`"motherduck"`) — connectors are shared organizational state, not
per-user.

v1ConnectorAuthType.description:
ConnectorAuthType declares how a connector acquires per-user
credentials.

 - CONNECTOR_AUTH_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Proto3 default. Conforming implementations should not return this.
 - CONNECTOR_AUTH_TYPE_OAUTH: Standard OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. Admin provisions
client_id/client_secret out-of-band (typically in Secrets under a
convention path); users complete an interactive authorization
flow; per-user tokens are stored and auto-refreshed by the
implementation's Secrets backend.
 - CONNECTOR_AUTH_TYPE_STATIC_TOKEN: Long-lived bearer token pasted in by each user (for example a
MotherDuck PAT). No admin OAuth app. Token is stored in Secrets
and set as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on the MCP request
verbatim — no refresh flow.

configured.description:
Whether the connector is backed by admin credentials in Secrets.
For OAuth connectors this means client_id + client_secret are
present in the Secrets backend. For static-token connectors this
is always true (no admin setup is required).

connected.description:
Whether the caller user has successfully authorized the
connector — i.e. Secrets holds a usable credential under the
per-user convention. Only populated when the request carries a
user_id.

v1ConnectorCatalogEntry.description:
ConnectorCatalogEntry is a single row in ListConnectors — the
Connector plus per-user, per-catalog state.

description.description: Description used by the SDK for delegation matching.

v1CreateAgentRequest.description: CreateAgentRequest is the request message for CreateAgent.

v1CreateAgentResponse.description: CreateAgentResponse is the response message for CreateAgent.

name.description: Display identifier. Unique across the control plane.

mcpAllowedTools.description: Tool name wildcards allowed on this connector.

authProviderRef.description: Optional auth-provider hint for the Secrets backend.

mcpDisallowedTools.description: Tool names the agent must NOT invoke. See `Connector.mcp_disallowed_tools`.

v1CreateConnectorRequest.description:
CreateConnectorRequest is the request message for CreateConnector.
Admin-only; writes a shared organizational resource.

v1CreateConnectorResponse.description: CreateConnectorResponse is the response message for CreateConnector.

description.description: Description used by the SDK for delegation matching.

v1CreateSkillRequest.description: CreateSkillRequest is the request message for CreateSkill.

v1CreateSkillResponse.description: CreateSkillResponse is the response message for CreateSkill.

v1DeleteAgentResponse.description:
DeleteAgentResponse is the response message for DeleteAgent. It is
intentionally empty.

v1DeleteConnectorResponse.description:
DeleteConnectorResponse is the response message for DeleteConnector.
It is intentionally empty.

v1DeleteSkillResponse.description:
DeleteSkillResponse is the response message for DeleteSkill. It is
intentionally empty.

v1DisconnectConnectorResponse.description:
DisconnectConnectorResponse is the response message for
DisconnectConnector. It is intentionally empty.

v1GetAgentResponse.description: GetAgentResponse is the response message for GetAgent.

v1GetSkillResponse.description: GetSkillResponse is the response message for GetSkill.

v1GetUserConfigResponse.description: GetUserConfigResponse is the response message for GetUserConfig.

nextPageToken.description: Opaque pagination token. Empty when there are no further results.

v1ListAgentsResponse.description: ListAgentsResponse is the response message for ListAgents.

entries.description: Matching connector catalog entries on this page.

v1ListConnectorsResponse.description: ListConnectorsResponse is the response message for ListConnectors.

v1ListSkillsResponse.description: ListSkillsResponse is the response message for ListSkills.

headers.description:
HTTP headers to send with every MCP request. Typically contains
`Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

v1McpServerConfig.description:
McpServerConfig is one entry in the `mcp_servers` map a worker
passes to the agent SDK. `transport` is derived from `url` by the
Config Dispenser (see `config.md` §7); `headers` carries the
per-user `Authorization` header materialized from Secrets.

v1McpTransport.description:
McpTransport is the wire protocol an MCP server speaks.

The transport is NOT stored on Connector — it is derived from the
connector URL at resolve time, so the connector catalog does not
carry transport metadata. See `config.md` §7 for the derivation
rule.

 - MCP_TRANSPORT_UNSPECIFIED: Proto3 default. Conforming implementations should not emit this.
 - MCP_TRANSPORT_HTTP: Streamable HTTP transport.
 - MCP_TRANSPORT_SSE: Server-Sent Events transport.

v1SetConnectorStaticTokenResponse.description:
SetConnectorStaticTokenResponse is the response message for
SetConnectorStaticToken. It is intentionally empty.

name.description:
Skill identifier. Unique within the owner's collection. Used as
the directory name under `.claude/skills/`.

description.description:
Human-readable description. The agent SDK matches delegation
targets on this text.

content.description:
Full SKILL.md text, including the YAML frontmatter block the SDK
parses.

createdAt.description: Time the skill was first created.

updatedAt.description: Time the skill was most recently updated.

v1Skill.description:
Skill is a per-user skill definition. Unlike Agents, skills must be
filesystem artifacts at `.claude/skills/{name}/SKILL.md` at run time
— the agent SDK has no programmatic API for skills. Workers hydrate
`content` to the workspace filesystem at run start.

v1SkillDefinition.description:
SkillDefinition is the shape a worker consumes when hydrating a
skill onto the run workspace filesystem.

authorizeUrl.description: URL the caller should redirect the user's browser to.

state.description:
Opaque state token the caller must pass back to
CompleteConnectorOAuthFlow to prove the callback corresponds to
this flow. Implementations typically encode user_id and a nonce.

v1StartConnectorOAuthFlowResponse.description:
StartConnectorOAuthFlowResponse is the response message for
StartConnectorOAuthFlow.

v1UpdateAgentResponse.description: UpdateAgentResponse is the response message for UpdateAgent.

v1UpdateSkillResponse.description: UpdateSkillResponse is the response message for UpdateSkill.

agents.description: All sub-agent definitions this user owns.

skills.description: All skill definitions this user owns.

allowedMcpTools.description:
Tool-name wildcards to add to the agent SDK's allowed-tools list.
The union of `mcp_allowed_tools` across every entry in
`mcp_servers`.

disallowedMcpTools.description:
Tool names to add to the agent SDK's disallowed-tools list. The
union of `mcp_disallowed_tools` across every entry in `mcp_servers`.

v1UserConfig.description:
UserConfig is the materialized runtime configuration for a user,
returned by GetUserConfig. It captures a point-in-time snapshot —
implementations MAY re-resolve on every call or cache briefly.
